summary.intent: Address PR review findings on the NumberInput component by fixing edge cases with infinite bounds, NaN/Inf values, and precision overflow, then commit, push, and resolve the review threads on GitHub.
summary.outcome: All three review findings were fixed (infinite bound no-ops, is_finite guard on SyncValue, precision cap in round_to_precision), 6 new tests added bringing the suite to 2005, all 10 CI gates passed, and changes were committed and pushed.

repo: CI gate suite is called xci-fast and enforces 10 gates including clippy
PR review passes are numbered (this was pass 9), suggesting an iterative review workflow on this PR

code.finding: IncrementToMax/DecrementToMin must guard against non-finite bounds to avoid producing ±inf values
SyncValue handler must apply is_finite guard before clamp/round to reject NaN and Inf inputs, consistent with other value setters
round_to_precision casts precision to i32 for powi; large precision values can overflow or produce infinite factors — precision must be capped to a safe range before the cast

workflow: Test coverage is treated as a hard requirement: new fixes must be accompanied by new tests
The flow is fix → test locally → clippy → xci-fast → commit/push → reply inline on GitHub and mark resolved
